sections.title: A: Write one word in each gap
sections.instructions: Write one word in each gap.

items.sentence: One of (1) ...... websites that has been very successful in recent years is eBay. On eBay, people take things that belong (2) ...... them and offer them for sale. Other people offer (3) ...... amount of money, and (4) ...... person who offers the most money wins the item. They then pay (5) ...... the item (6) ...... cheque or credit card. It's (7) ...... simple idea, but it's become a very popular way of buying and selling. Even if you only have a (8) ...... of money, you can often find something you want on eBay. Most of the items are (9) ...... good condition, and eBay has a (10) ...... of happy users.

sections.title: B: Word formation
sections.instructions: Complete by changing the form of the word in capitals when this is necessary.

items.sentence: I've made my ...... . (DECIDE). I'm going to buy the blue one.
This dress is wonderful – but it's a little too ...... for me. (EXPENSE)
I've broken my new CD player and now it's ...... ! (USE)
In the past, rich people often had ...... to do everything for them. (SERVE)
We offer top quality products at ...... prices! (AFFORD)
The advert was ...... . (TRUE), so I complained to the manager.
Please be careful with that painting – it's extremely ...... . (VALUE)
When you ...... our prices with other shops, we're the cheapest! (COMPARE)

sections.title: C: Key Word Transformation
sections.instructions: Complete each second sentence using the word given, so that it has a similar meaning to the first sentence. Write between two and five words.

items.sentence:
Could you lend some money to me until the weekend? **from**
Could I ...... you until the weekend?

I can't decide which shoes I like most, the blue ones or the green ones. **choose**
I can't ...... the blue shoes and the green shoes.

Why don't you return the sweater to the shop you got it from? **back**
Why don't you ...... to the shop you got it from?

It can be very worrying when you owe money to the bank. **debt**
It can be very worrying when you ...... to the bank.

We need to go quickly or the shops will be closed. **up**
We need to ...... or the shops will be closed.

I paid for the CD using notes and coins and then left the shop. **cash**
I paid for the CD ...... and then left the shop.
Would you like ...... cup of coffee?
She is ...... honest person.
I haven't got ...... money left.
There were ...... people at the concert – it was almost empty.
Could I have ...... water, please?
He is ...... best player in the team.
We need ...... eggs to make this cake.
There is ...... information on this website – it's really useful.
He only has ...... friends, but they are very close.
I'd like ...... advice about the best route to take.
She goes to ...... school near her house.
There isn't ...... milk left. We need to buy some more.

sections.title: E: Complete the sentences
sections.instructions: Write one word in each gap.

items.sentence: I'm going to ...... a look at the new shop in the high street.
Can I try this jacket ...... ? I want to see if it fits.
This laptop is ...... sale for half price this week only!
I can't ...... my mind about which phone to buy.
The shoes are too ...... – I'll have to take them back to the shop.
She got a great ...... – she paid half the usual price.
He paid ...... cash – he didn't use a card.
I'd like to ...... a complaint – this product stopped working after one day.
The shop offers a full ...... if you're not satisfied.
They were ...... out of stock of my size, so I ordered it online.
She saved ...... for months before she could afford the new laptop.
He spent a ...... of money on new clothes this month.
We got this at a ...... – it was much cheaper than normal.
You should always ...... prices before you buy something expensive.
